story_summary: A career auditor recounts multiple instances of fraud and ethical breaches encountered over decades, describing investigative actions, escalation choices, and reflections on professional values.

context.stakes: criminal prosecution, jail, large financial losses (tens of millions), reputational damage, client termination

core_conflict: Whether to investigate and escalate suspected financial misconduct despite professional, interpersonal, and client-related risks.

decision_moments.trigger: Discovery of bank deposit discrepancies at a physician group client (Billie Pullen case).
constraints: Client relationships and embarrassment of staff
Limited immediate documentary evidence until review
options: Inform the client/owner and involve authorities
decision_moments.choice: Reported findings to the physician (Dr. Martin) who called the police; the bookkeeper was arrested.
decision_moments.reasoning: The discrepancies were clear and required authoritative action to stop theft and correct records.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: Potential damage to client relationships and personal discomfort in exposing an employee.
decision_moments.outcome: The bookkeeper was arrested; subsequent family tragedy (husband's suicide reported in newspaper).

decision_moments.trigger: Recurring secondary endorsement signatures and suspicion about livestock purchase payments (Ham Stockett case).
constraints: Initial plausible explanation from the implicated employee
decision_moments.choice: Flowcharted cattle numbers and weights for a month and reconciled purchases to slaughterhouse numbers; then reported findings to the company president.
decision_moments.reasoning: A nagging sixth sense indicated something was awry; systematic reconciliation could confirm fraud.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: Time and effort of an extended investigation; confronting an employee and management.
decision_moments.outcome: Employee confessed and was fired on the spot.

decision_moments.trigger: Suspicious shorter cancelled checks and odd endorsements at a municipal bond dealer (Alvis & Company).
constraints: Need for bank documentation to verify suspicions
options: Order bank duplicates and examine microfiche
Confront the bookkeeper without supporting documentation
decision_moments.choice: Ordered duplicates from the bank’s microfiche files and investigated further, uncovering an endorsement-clipping scheme.
decision_moments.reasoning: Physical differences in checks suggested deliberate manipulation; bank duplicates would reveal the original endorsements.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: Resource/time investment and potential escalation if fraud proven.
decision_moments.outcome: Uncovered an intricate embezzlement scheme by the bookkeeper.

decision_moments.trigger: Management's refusal to adjust materially misleading financial transactions (Documation, Inc.).
constraints: CEO's aggressive denial and public posture
Potential client conflict and legal exposure
options: Accept management's refusal and remain silent
Persist in negotiating adjustments with management
Escalate the issue to the board of directors
decision_moments.choice: Presented audit findings to Documation’s board of directors after management refused to adjust the numbers.
decision_moments.reasoning: Management's refusal left the board as the proper authority to correct and hold leadership accountable.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: Professional exposure, loss of the client engagement (Peat Marwick was later dismissed by the board).
decision_moments.outcome: The CEO was terminated by the board; subsequently the firm was removed as auditor by the same board.

decision_moments.trigger: A senior partner at a law firm confided knowledge of money laundering and skimming.
constraints: Extremely confidential nature of the information
High-profile reputations involved and legal implications
options: Advise silence to protect the firm's reputation
Advise contacting appropriate legal authority immediately
decision_moments.choice: Advised the partner to personally contact the U.S. Attorney General; he made the call immediately.
decision_moments.reasoning: The severity and criminal nature of the allegations required reporting to legal authorities rather than only internal handling.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: Potential professional discomfort and involvement in a high-profile legal matter.
decision_moments.outcome: The partner called the U.S. Attorney General and then went to Washington, D.C.; narrator and partner did not discuss it further.

principles.evidence: the accounting profession trains one to be skeptical and vigilant.
I flowcharted the number and weight of the cattle, from the buying lot to the kill floor, for a month.

principles.principle: Escalate material misstatements to appropriate authority when management resists correction.
principles.evidence: I attended a meeting of Documation’s board of directors and presented our findings.

principles.principle: Value confidentiality when counseling individuals facing sensitive disclosures.
principles.evidence: He wanted to talk con fidentially with someone he trusted

principles.principle: Question the fairness of punitive actions and consider proportionality.
principles.evidence: I have always questioned the fairness of the firing.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: When numeric or transactional anomalies appear, map the transaction flow end-to-end to reconcile quantities and identify manipulation.
decision_heuristics.evidence: I flowcharted the number and weight of the cattle, from the buying lot to the kill floor, for a month.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: If physical documents show inconsistencies, obtain bank duplicates or originals to compare endorsements and verify authenticity.
decision_heuristics.evidence: including ordering duplicates from the bank’s microfiche files, we uncovered another intricate scheme.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Persist in requesting management adjustments; if refused, escalate the issue to the board for resolution.
decision_heuristics.evidence: I worked for days trying to get them to adjust the numbers, but Ray staunchly refused. So, I attended a meeting of Documation’s board of directors and presented our findings.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: When a trusted colleague discloses criminal conduct, advise immediate contact with the appropriate legal authority.
decision_heuristics.evidence: We agreed that he should personally contact the U.S. Attorney General and describe what he knew. He made the phone call right then and there.

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Do not ignore small recurring discrepancies; even modest shortages can indicate long-term or systemic abuse.
decision_heuristics.evidence: I calculated that this shortage amounted to about $9 for every year this lady had worked.

leadership_patterns.risk_tolerance: Low tolerance for ethical compromise; willing to accept personal and firm-level professional risk to expose wrongdoing.
leadership_patterns.communication_style: Direct and evidence-based; willing to escalate to boards and authorities and to provide confidential counsel.
leadership_patterns.accountability_posture: Proactive investigator and escalator of issues; holds individuals and management accountable through formal channels.
leadership_patterns.ethical_framing: Values-driven and duty-based; frames decisions around integrity, legal compliance, and professional responsibility.

quotes.quote: Oh, he knows I took the money —or he wouldn’t have you out here checking my books!
Let’s get that bastard over here and see what he has to say!
Gentleman, this man is a lying son of a bitch!
We have opted to believe Peat Marwick.
